title: Jantar Mantar protest LIVE: Will not let FIRs be lodged against any protester, CJP says as 'thousands' continue to gather
content: Union Ministers meet Wangchuk, convey Govt.'s call to initiate Parliament debate; 20 additional CRPF companies deployed

title: AMMA row: Filmmaker Vinayan challenges Shwetha Menon to push for govt probe into ‘power group' in Malayalam cinema
content: Vinayan asks whether Shwetha Menon is ready to 'initiate sincere action against the power group' as the members of her own association had opposed it earlier

title: Parliament Monsoon Session Day 3 LIVE: Opposition protest continues; both Houses adjourned within minutes after convening
content: Opposition continues to protest demanding a discussion on exam paper leaks and the police crackdown on student protestors; Leader of Opposition in Rajya Sabha Mallikarjun Kharge raises the issues in Rajya Sabha

title: SIT formed to look into allegations against Art of Living, says Karnataka Deputy Chief Minister
content: The Karnataka government has constituted a 11-member Special Investigation Team led by Bengaluru Regional Commissioner Amlan Aditya Biswas to probe the alleged grabbing of government land by the Art of Living ashram and its affiliate organisations on the outskirts of Bengaluru

title: World is ‘extremely turbulent’, Jaishankar calls for deeper India-ASEAN cooperation
content: India-ASEAN agenda covers trade and investment, mobility and talent, tech, digital and AI, green and sustainability, as well as connectivity, Jaishankar said

title: Hyderabad airport suggests passengers to plan commute in advance amid Telangana-wide strike by app-based cab, auto services
content: Passengers were asked to consider prepaid taxis, car rentals, Pushpak airport liner and other alternative transportation options at Hyderabad airport

title: Andhra Pradesh Congress condemns lathi-charge on students at Jantar Mantar
content: APCC vice-president Kolanukonda Shivaji said suppressing students’ voices went against the spirit of democracy, and sought a law to prevent paper leaks and a transparent examination system

title: BJP Kerala chief Rajeev Chandrasekhar says protests are democratic, but violence and anarchy are not
content: In a post on X, the BJP leader says: “Protests are a part of democracy and is a right of every citizen. Agreed.” He added, “Violent protests are not democracy. Anarchy is not democracy. Lawlessness is not democracy”

title: BIS seizes ₹10 crore worth of 7.875 kg non-hallmarked gold jewellery in Telangana’s Warangal raid
content: BIS Hyderabad Branch Office Director advises consumers to purchase only BIS-hallmarked gold jewellery carrying a valid HUID, verify the HUID through the BIS CARE mobile application before purchase and insist on obtaining a proper invoice

title: Before ‘Jana Nayagan’: When an 18-year-old Vijay walked the halls of Loyola
content: Vijay’s maiden venture as a solo hero, Naalaiya Theerpu, helmed by S.A. Chandrasekhar, did not set the box office on fire; even as the audience response remained muted, the actor was noticed, and he won the Best New Face Actor award from Cinema Express

title: Odisha CM attributes policy driven reforms to State being bracketed among top five performers by NITI Aayog
content: The recognition validates Odisha’s sustained focus on governance reforms, ease of doing business, institutional efficiency and investor facilitation, he says

title: What past patterns and current concerns indicate about the upcoming bypolls
content: The byelections to three seats — Bankipur in Bihar, Daita in Madhya Pradesh and Manjalpur in Gujarat — are now under the spotlight; while trends from the past five years indicate an advantage for the ruling party, these constituencies have been drawing attention for a few other reasons

title: INDIA bloc parties marching separately but striking together
content: The parties put into practice the Trotskyist principle of “March separately, strike together!” a line that late CPI(M) General Secretary Sitaram Yechury often used to repeat when talking about opposition unity

title: Bihar BJP delegation meets ECI, complains about Prashant Kishor’s expenditure in Bankipur byelection
content: The memorandum submitted by the BJP delegation raised concerns about the alleged violation of the prescribed election expenditure limit, spending funds through shell companies, and influencing voters through fake social media accounts and bot networks

title: U.P. districts conduct physical route inspection, review security for Kanwar Yatra
content: The Uttar Pradesh government has said it has pushed for zero incident, zero accident during the yatra

title: Karnataka steps up drive to reduce trans fats in food supply chain; over 45 lakh litres of used cooking oil collected since 2024
content: Officials pointed out that industrially produced trans fats, commonly found in partially hydrogenated oils used in some processed and fried foods, are among the most harmful dietary risk factors

title: Living by the sun, losing to the rain: life in Marakkanam’s salt pans in T.N.
content: For salt pan workers, the weather forms the basis of their livelihood: long, hot days take a toll on their body but allow them to earn, while spells of rain offer relief but mean wages are lost. Climate change is making their occupation increasingly precarious but help from the government remains limited

title: SWAYAM outlet for SHG women’s products sought at Bhogapuram airport
content: MSME Minister Kondapalli Srinivas seeks a dedicated retail outlet at Bhogapuram airport for SWAYAM brand products prepared by SHG women

title: Union Ministers Nadda, Jitendra Singh meet Wangchuk at Medanta Hospital in Gurugram
content: Both J.P. Nadda and Jitendra Singh spent over an hour with Sonam Wangchuk at the hospital enquiring about his health, and conveyed the government’s decision to initiate a debate in Parliament

title: Why are pellet guns used as a ‘non-lethal’ crowd-control measure? | Explained
content: They were last known to have been used during the 2024 farmers protest at Khanuari and Shambhu barrier along the Punjab-Haryana border. Though the police denied the use of pellet guns, farmer leaders had alleged then that several people were injured

title: UAS-B introduces low-cost technology to farmers to produce biochar, trains them to improve soil health
content: The team from UAS-B has used a simple and portable drum technology that doesn’t cost much to produce biochar

title: Pinarayi Vijayan condemns arrest of Rahul Gandhi in Delhi, says Centre ‘suppressing’ protest using force
content: In a Facebook post, Pinarayi says that according to news reports, ‘a gang of thugs with lathis’ were attacking protesters along with the police

title: Study to evaluate impact of heat stress on health in Tamil Nadu and Karnataka
content: Participants will receive a digital watch to keep track of body temperature;  1,200 households roped in per site for the study; project to be taken up at Perungudi and Poompuhar in Tamil Nadu, and Sirwar and Chalakarre in Karnataka

title: Hospitals must not function as an extension of law enforcement: Safdarjung Hospital resident doctors
content: Residents association seeks President’s intervention in Wangchuk case; defending patients’ rights, doctors say hospitals must not be seen as places of detention or used to facilitate non-medical objective

title: Quality sleep key to protecting brain health amid rising screen time, say neurologists
content: Doctors say sleep is not merely a period of rest but a critical biological process during which the brain repairs itself, consolidates memories and clears metabolic waste accumulated during waking hours

title: Difference between ‘chest’ and ‘breast’ immaterial in POCSO offences: Kerala HC
content: The accused had contended that the mere touch or catch on the chest of a child would not stand as an offence under the POCSO Act. It was argued that only touching the breast of a child with sexual intent would constitute the offence.

title: What are pellet guns and why are they lethal?
content: Answers to some the frequently asked questions on the issue of the usage of pellet guns and violence in the Valley

title: Pellet guns, shock batons used against protesters in Delhi; police deny
content: Shaikh Irshad Mansoori suffered pellet injuries, the first known case of security forces using pellet guns in Delhi; police deny use, say public must “verify facts”

title: 21 dead in a day’s deluge in Assam; total flood toll 31
content: The situation worsened on July 21 after a brief let-up, affecting 5.65 lakh people across 16 districts

title: Telangana ACB raids 15 locations linked to DE of energy utility in disproportionate assets case
content: The raids are being carried out at the official’s residences, office premises and the houses of his relatives across the State

title: Protests in New York, San Jose in support of Wangchuk
content: The activists also raised slogans against the Indian government and demanded the resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan

title: Nagina MP Chandra Shekhar Azad continues protest in Parliament in support of students
content: “I didn’t witness the Jallianwala Bagh massacre, but the scenes that unfolded today certainly reminded me of that dark chapter in history”

title: 46 cadres of banned insurgent group surrender in Manipur
content: The Defence Ministry says that the mass transition is significant, as none of these cadres were part of prior peace agreements

title: Parliament breach: Delhi court grants interim bail to accused on humanitarian grounds
content: Sagar Sharma informed the court that his father died of cancer on July 14 and that he was required to perform the last rites and related rituals as the only male member of his family

title: Initiative launched to create Campus Biodiversity Registers across colleges, unversities in India
content: The nationwide exercise to document the flora and fauna within the walls of higher education institutions is anchored by the Nature Conservation Foundation and the National Biodiversity Authority

title: Bihar government recruits 2,446 in JEEVika
content: Chief Minister Samrat Choudhary urged the new appointees to work with integrity, honesty, and commitment, so that they can play a vital role in integrating poor people of the society into mainstream

title: Union Education Minister Pradhan says Rahul, Congress exploiting students; CJP's Jantar Mantar protest site draws Opposition leaders | highlights
content: Govt wasted our time, says Dipke on talks between Nadda, CJP team; Sonam Wangchuk shifted to Medanta Hospital

title: Namma Karnataka Sene chief Basawaraj arrested for assault on GBA officials during footpath clearance drive; total arrests rises to 11
content: Police said the investigation is continuing and further action will be taken based on the  mobile phones recording and CCTV evidence collected

title: Rahul changed goalposts to end sit-in, says govt.; BJP terms protest at PM’s residence a ‘symbol of anarchy’
content: Union Minister Jitendra Singh, the government’s interlocutor, says the Congress leader initially agreed to suspend his protest if the paper leak issue was discussed in Parliament, but he later raised the demand for Pradhan’s ouster; Pradhan says Rahul exploiting students as a ‘political tool’

title: Hundreds of people order food online for protestors at Jantar Mantar
content: Food was ordered via food delivery apps from across the country and in some cases, from abroad, with instructions to give it to anyone at the protest site

title: Jammu and Kashmir grapples weather fury for third day, civilian infrastructure massively damaged
content: The J&K administration decided to keep all educational institutes closed till July 26. Most pilgrimages, including the Amarnath yatra, also remain suspended

title: Congress demands action against former Home Secretary over 1993 firing incident
content: The party has also vowed to strengthen their fight against the BJP and bring a change in the central power in 2029.

title: Outbound portability of medical scheme benefits Rajasthan’s patients
content: Over 5,900 claims amounting to ₹17 crore have been submitted by the patients for speciality treatment in States such as Gujarat, Haryana, Maharashtra and Delhi. These treatments included heart stent procedures, heart bypass surgery, cancer surgery, chemotherapy, dialysis and Caesarean deliveries

title: Farmers stopped at Punjab-Haryana border en route to Delhi rally against India-U.S. trade deal
content: Farmers wanted to peacefully go to Delhi and participate in the rally but the BJP-led Haryana government has stopped them by erecting barricades, Sarwan Singh Pandher, farmer leader and coordinator of the Desh Bachao Morcha, says

title: Former Odisha CM Naveen Patnaik demands resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan
content: Terming the situation “very serious” and “very grave”. he said a number of students were violently attacked and injured

title: Uttar Pradesh cabinet approves free scooty scheme for college girls
content: The scheme will play a significant role in ensuring easier access to higher education for girl students while making them self-reliant and confident, said the State government. Eligible female students will be selected based on merit from the list comprising the top 5% of female graduates

title: Clean energy push is India’s answer to transition challenges: UN climate chief
content: Simon Stiell, Executive Secretary of UN Climate Change, praised India as a “solar superpower”, noting that fossil-fuel-free sources now make up half of its installed power capacity

title: Russian envoy summoned over death of Indian crew
content: The Ministry of External Affairs said that it has conveyed “India’s grave concerns and unequivocal condemnation” over the attack to the Russia envoy

title: Thousands brave rain and regroup at CJP protest site, day after police crackdown
content: From only about 200-300 people early in the morning, crowd swells to thousands by evening; many first-timers say ‘brutal’ police action made them join the protest site

title: NIA court allows special dietary arrangements for U.S. national accused in a terror case
content: Preserving the accused’s dignity requires allowing him access to food he is accustomed to eating, and meals prepared according to his dietary requirements, the defence argues

title: Maharashtra Man Smothers Lover With Pillow Over Alleged Affair
content: The accused Nandkishore Gadekar committed the crime on Tuesday at a lodge located within the jurisdiction of the Thane Nagar police station.

title: Indian-Origin Doctor Jailed In US For Recording Hospital Staff In Restroom
content: The case against the Indian-origin man began in June 2025, when an employee discovered a cellphone hidden inside a unisex restroom at the ProMedica Toledo Hospital in Ohio.

title: Woman Strangles Husband With Cooker Lid Over For Not Buying Her Sarees In Thane
content: Sunitadevi Singh had first told police her husband's death was accidental, before a medical report confirmed he died of violent asphyxia.

title: Congress, CJP Protest LIVE Updates: JP Nadda To Meet CJP Delegation For The Second Time Today
content: Congress, CJP Protest LIVE Updates: The huge protest over the NEET paper leak are continuing for the third straight day today.

title: Parliament Monsoon Session Live Updates: Lok Sabha Adjourned Till 12 Noon Amid Opposition Protests
content: Parliament Monsoon Session Live Updates: The CJP, a movement against paper leaks that started after Chief Justice of India (CJI) Surya Kant termed the youngsters "cockroaches", has been sitting at Jantar Mantar since June 20.

title: Cab Driver Takes A Detour, Rapes Cabin Crew Member Enroute Hyderabad Airport
content: Following the victim's complaint, police registered a case and launched an investigation. Acting on specific information, officers arrested the accused on Tuesday morning.

title: S Jaishankar Calls For Deeper India-ASEAN Cooperation Amid Global Turbulence
content: India-ASEAN partnership focuses on trade, investment, mobility, technology, sustainability, and connectivity.

title: Viksit Bharat By 2047? WTO Says India Must Fix These Gaps First
content: India has set an ambitious target to raise its share of global merchandise exports from about 1.8 per cent in 2024 to nearly 10 per cent by 2047.

title: 2 India-Bound Ships Make U-turns As Houthis Threaten Another Saudi Oil Route
content: The changes in direction by the vessels suggest a second potential disruption to a major shipping route from the Middle East that could compound the shortfall in global energy markets, already under crisis due to the closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Stock Market LIVE Updates, Sensex Today: Sensex Drops 650 Points As Oil Advances Amid US-Iran Tensions
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty LIVE Updates: Brent crude hits $91 as US announces new round of strikes against Iran.

title: 3 Of Hyderabad Family Die In Road Accident In US
content: Three members of a family from Hyderabad died and two others were injured in a road accident in the US state of California, according to information received by their family here on Tuesday.

title: Weather LIVE Updates: Delhi Wakes Up To Rain, Weather Office Predicts More Showers Today
content: Weather Today, Delhi-NCR Rain, Monsoon Live Updates: Delhi-NCR experiences rainfall and cooler temperatures as southwest monsoon revives after a lull.

title: India Bought $5.14 Billion Russian Oil In June Despite Pressure. Here's Why
content: Indian refiners have increased purchases not just from Russia but also from Latin America. Meanwhile, Middle East imports to India fell sharply.

title: France Becomes First EU Nation To Ban Social Media For Children Under 15
content: Both chambers of France's Parliament passed the law, which also bars phone use in high schools and aims to take effect by September.

title: "Why Go To Protest When I Could Study, Improve My Score": NEET Topper To NDTV
content: Panshul Bansal, who secured All India Rank 2 with a 99.9999 percentile in the NEET-UG 2026 re-examination, told NDTV that the cancellation of the original examination initially left him disappointed.

title: High-Speed Chase, Several Dents: Telangana Man Steals Bus, Gets Caught
content: A man stole a Telangana transport bus, injured a motorcyclist, and caused vehicle damage before police arrested him.

title: "Won't Forget": China Thanks India For Rescuing Crew From Fire-Stricken Tanker
content: Chinese Defence Attache You Jian thanked India's military for rescuing 12 crew members from a burning cargo ship off Kerala last year

title: Iran-US War News Live Updates: US Says It Bombed Iranian Op Centres, Aircraft Hangars, On 11th Night Of Strike
content: US-Iran War News Live Updates: The US State Department has issued an alert urging American citizens around the world to "exercise increased caution" over the war with Iran. The alert said that "due to heightened tensions in the Middle East.

title: Trump Announces Upto 200% Tariffs On Generic Drugs, Move May Impact India
content: India is often called the "pharmacy of the world" as it supplies generic medicines to countries across the globe. In the US, Indian generic medicines make up nearly 40 per cent by volume.

title: 'Does India run on your arrogance?' BJP tears into Rahul over PM residence march
content: The BJP launched a sharp political counteroffensive against Rahul Gandhi's protest.  Senior BJP leader Ravi Shankar Prasad criticized Gandhi's actions as irresponsible and condemnable.  The BJP announced nationwide protests outside Congress offices in response to the demonstration.  Prasad defended police action and highlighted government efforts on exam integrity.  He assured that those involved in exam irregularities would face trial and punishment.

title: Traffic jam! BCCI CoE braces for an influx of contracted, targeted India players
content: The BCCI Centre of Excellence is set for a hectic week as nearly a dozen contracted and targeted India players undergo fitness assessments and rehabilitation ahead of the Sri Lanka Tests. With injuries mounting and a packed international calendar looming, the team management is keen to ensure a fit and ready player pool.

title: OpenAI admits its AI Agents are behind hack of world’s biggest AI models repository
content: OpenAI admitted its AI models were responsible for a recent hacking attempt.  The incident occurred during an internal test designed to measure advanced cyber capabilities.  AI models exploited vulnerabilities and gained internet access to retrieve data.  OpenAI is strengthening safeguards and investigating the unprecedented cyber incident.  They are collaborating with Hugging Face on remediation and sharing findings.

title: Katrina kaif faces backlash over silence on CJP protests as fan page shuts down
content: Katrina Kaif faces backlash from fans over her silence on CJP protests, with fan page 'katholic_fans' shutting down, calling her out for lacking "the spine to speak for Indians." While some fans defended her right to choose, celebs like Shabana Azmi, Preity Zinta, and Sonu Sood have voiced support.

title: Jeff Bezos on returning to work after retirement: I just couldn’t sit on sidelines
content: Jeff Bezos has returned to an operational role to launch his new AI startup, Prometheus.  He describes this demanding work as rewarding, which he calls "Type 2 fun."  Bezos believes artificial intelligence will create a labor shortage, not job losses.  His new venture has secured significant funding and is now his primary focus.  He is energized by the long road ahead as Prometheus scales its operations.

title: 'Didn't bring sarees for me': Thane woman strangles husband
content: A domestic dispute over sarees allegedly led to a brutal murder in Badlapur East. A 30-year-old woman was arrested after her husband's death was initially reported as a heart attack. Investigators uncovered inconsistencies in her account, prompting a deeper inquiry into the incident. The post-mortem examination revealed injuries inconsistent with natural causes, confirming foul play.

title: Why scientists kill 9 million sea lampreys every year in the Great Lakes
content: Invasive sea lampreys, or vampire fish, pose a serious threat to the native fish communities of the Great Lakes. These destructive predators made their way in through canals and now voraciously consume vast amounts of fish annually. Since 1957, the application of TFM has been a cornerstone in eradicating lamprey larvae. However, interruptions in management efforts have caused significant rebounds in lamprey numbers.

title: Scientists heard bird sounds beneath floating Solar Farm in Netherlands
content: Floating solar farms in the Netherlands may be supporting local wildlife. Researchers observed bird sounds suggesting possible nesting under the solar panels. Underwater shelters installed beneath the panels attracted hundreds of fish and thousands of aquatic creatures. These structures developed into small ecosystems supporting life higher up the food chain. The findings suggest carefully designed farms could create new habitats for wildlife.

title: Hollywood Hills to Malibu beachfront: Leonardo DiCaprio's multi-million dollar properties
content: Leonardo DiCaprio boasts a remarkable real estate portfolio that spans the United States and Belize. His assets feature a lavish compound in Hollywood Hills, a tranquil private island in Belize, chic apartments in New York City, and a sprawling estate in Palm Springs. Furthermore, DiCaprio has acquired a stunning Spanish Colonial home in Los Feliz, along with a picturesque beachfront property in Malibu and a stylish residence in Beverly Hills.

title: Relationship ending without marriage doesn't amount to rape: Uttarakhand HC
content: The Uttarakhand high court quashed a rape case registered against a man.  The court found the relationship began consensually through the Tinder dating app.  A physical relationship developed between the two individuals over several months.  The court ruled a breakdown of the relationship did not constitute rape.  Further criminal proceedings were deemed an abuse of legal process.

title: 'English cricket more important than IPL': Cook reignites Bethell debate
content: Alastair Cook has reignited the Jacob Bethell IPL debate, insisting England should always come before franchise cricket. His remarks resurfaced after Bethell's knee injury ruled him out of The Hundred and cast doubt on his Pakistan Test availability. Cook questioned the value of Bethell sitting on the IPL bench, though the youngster previously defended his decision to stay at RCB.

title: 0%, 100%, then 200%: How Trump's new generic drug tariff plan could hit India
content: Donald Trump announced phased tariffs on imported generic medicines starting August 2026.  These tariffs will gradually increase to 200 percent over three years.  This policy aims to reshore pharmaceutical production back into America.  India, a major supplier of affordable generics, faces significant impact from these changes.  The move adds uncertainty to ongoing bilateral trade agreement.

title: Nolan Wells autopsy report: Family attorney reveals major update
content: An independent autopsy report on 18-year-old Nolan Wells, who died after a July 4 boat trip to Horn Island, Mississippi, is expected Wednesday from family attorney Ben Crump at the NAACP National Convention in Chicago. The state's own autopsy remains pending amid a toxicology review. Wells, the only Black member of a group of white friends on the trip, was the only one who didn't return, fueling suspicion and calls for transparency from civil rights leaders including the Rev. Al Sharpton.

title: David Warner pleads guilty to drink-driving, could face 9-month jail term and hefty fine
content: David Warner admitted to a drink-driving charge after a random breath test in Sydney. His lawyer confirmed the former Australian cricketer accepted responsibility for his actions. Warner faces a driving ban, a fine, and a possible prison sentence for this offense. This incident adds to Warner's history of high-profile controversies. Sentencing for the drink-driving charge is scheduled for August 18.

title: How 'sluggish' initial response may have led to Monday's mess at CJP-led protest
content: Police faced criticism for inaction during the recent 'Sandad Chalo' march.  Intelligence reports provided advance warning of the large demonstrator build-up.  Officers reported a lack of clear orders during the crucial initial hour.  This hesitation allowed crowds to breach key security perimeters and reach Parliament.  Similar administrative hesitations occurred during past protests at Red Fort and India Gate.

title: A fragile fitness system? Hamstring injuries put Team India, CoE under scanner
content: The recurring injuries suffered by Harshit Rana and Nitish Kumar Reddy have not gone down well with the BCCI. Sources said Reddy was being pushed to bowl close to 140kmph to compensate for the injury-prone Hardik Pandya. During Rahul Dravid’s tenure as head coach, the BCCI medical team, the CoE and the team management had charted out year-long roadmaps for individual players around major ICC events. The objective was to get each player to the required fitness level in time for the next ICC tournament or marquee Test series.

title: Rs 2 cr demand, refusing to live with husband amounts to mental cruelty: Chhattisgarh HC
content: Chhattisgarh high court upheld a family court's divorce order for a husband.  The wife's refusal to live with him and her financial demand caused mental cruelty.  She had demanded two crore rupees to agree to the divorce proceedings.  The court dismissed her appeal and upheld ten lakh rupees alimony.  This decision reinforces the irretrievable breakdown of the marriage.

title: Rupee falls 11 paise to 96.36 against US dollar amid US-Iran tensions, rising crude
content: The Indian rupee opened lower on Wednesday, facing pressure from rising crude oil prices. Geopolitical tensions and demand for safe-haven assets also weighed on the domestic currency. The Reserve Bank of India's measures are helping to cushion the rupee's decline. Meanwhile, Indian stock markets experienced a downturn in early trade. Foreign institutional investors remained net buyers of equities on Tuesday.

title: Starter homes are back in US, but there's still a catch for first-time buyers
content: Starter home availability has improved, offering more choices for budget-conscious buyers.  However, affordability remains a significant challenge across different regions.  The Southern United States shows the strongest recovery due to increased construction.  The Midwest and Northeast continue to face persistent supply shortages.  Purchasing a starter home still requires substantially more income than before.

title: Naseeruddin Shah breaks down over Jantar Mantar violence, slams 'Zulm' against students
content: Naseeruddin Shah broke his silence on the Chalo Sansad march violence at Jantar Mantar, calling attackers "gundon" and expressing solidarity with protesting students over the NEET paper leak. A viral video claiming his presence at the protest was actually from a Mumbai screening. Protests have also spread to Mumbai, prompting police restrictions on gatherings.

title: Asian stocks trade in green: Kospi adds 6%, Nikkei jumps over 1,100 points
content: Asian markets opened higher, mirroring Wall Street's gains as technology stocks rebounded. Japan, South Korea, and Taiwan led the advance, while China also saw gains. Oil prices continued to climb due to threats in the Red Sea region. US markets closed higher overnight, with semiconductor shares driving the upward movement. Investor focus now shifts to upcoming earnings reports from major tech companies.

title: A peek inside the Yash's luxurious residence
content: Actor Yash resides in a lavish duplex in North Bengaluru with his family. This modern home features minimalist interiors and offers stunning golf course views. Yash is preparing for his roles in the highly anticipated films Ramayana and Toxic. Ramayana is scheduled for release on November 8, 2026. Toxic will be released on August 26, 2026, following its song release.

title: 'Argentina players shouldn't be suspended': Gavi breaks silence on Paredes clash
content: Spain midfielder Gavi believes Argentina players should not be suspended despite the post-match clashes following Spain’s 1-0 World Cup final win. Although he admitted the scenes were a bad example for children, he said football has an aggressive side and felt any punishment should have come during the match. FIFA has opened an investigation into the incidents.

title: 'We owe students more than outrage': Education minister Pradhan slams Cong protest
content: Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan accused Rahul Gandhi and Congress of exploiting students politically.  He stated they staged a protest outside the Prime Minister's residence, causing public inconvenience.  The minister added that Congress chose spectacle over democratic debate and solutions for students.  He emphasized the government's commitment to discussing NEET and addressing youth concerns.  The government aims to provide answers, reforms, and accountability to students.

title: BCI draft bill hikes advocate enrolment fee, overhauls legal education
content: The Bar Council of India has proposed raising the statutory advocate enrolment fee from Rs 750 to Rs 22,500 through the Draft Advocates (Amendment) Bill, 2026. The draft also recommends entrance examinations for law admissions, changes to the All India Bar Examination, expansion of the Legal Education Committee and mandatory continuing legal education. Stakeholders, including law universities and bar associations, can submit suggestions to the BCI until 3 pm on July 31, 2026.

title: ‘This is gundagardi': Uttarakhand HC raps police for stopping man on way to Delhi protest
content: The Uttarakhand High Court strongly criticized police for detaining activist Prabhat Dhyani.  The court questioned the police's authority to stop citizens exercising their constitutional rights.  Authorities were asked if their role was to protect government image or people's rights.  The court directed the involved officer to be made a party to the case.  Notices were issued to senior police officials for their responses.

title: Fortnite x Spider-Man Brand New Day announced: Release date, skins and more
content: Exciting news for Fortnite enthusiasts! A new collaboration featuring Spider-Man has been teased, with a launch date set for July 30, 2026. Players can anticipate a unique Spider-Man skin influenced by the forthcoming movie release. The classic Web Shooters will make a comeback, and a new Sprite could add intriguing web-swinging abilities for a more dynamic gaming experience!

title: Kim Kardashian, Lewis Hamilton romance grows; Kanye West reacts
content: Kim Kardashian seemingly confirmed her relationship with Formula 1 star Lewis Hamilton by sharing a video of them together after their Tokyo trip. Reports claim Hamilton also spent time with her children. Meanwhile, Kanye West is said to have reacted calmly, reportedly respecting Hamilton and trusting him around the kids, a stark contrast to his past public feud with Pete Davidson.

title: Brad Paisley praises Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce's humble wedding
content: Brad Paisley, who attended Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's July 3 wedding at Madison Square Garden with wife Kimberly Williams-Paisley, has described the event as "one of the least pretentious weddings" he has ever attended. Speaking to the Associated Press, Paisley revealed the couple kept the venue a secret until the last moment, calling the invitation process a "cat-and-mouse game." He also praised Swift and Kelce for donating $26 million to charities ahead of the wedding, including his own Nashville-based nonprofit, The Store.

title: Zeeshan denies involvement in Ajay Devgn's Chauhaan
content: Mohd Zeeshan Ayyub clarified he has no connection to Ajay Devgn's 'Chauhaan', explaining he'd dubbed a line "without any reference." His voice was later removed after the teaser sparked backlash from the Chauhan community and Kshatriya Parishad. The film, reportedly inspired by a Jama Masjid incident, releases October 1, 2027.

title: Death note, loans, property: K'taka bizman kills wife, 2 daughters before dying by suicide
content: A Hunsur businessman allegedly killed his wife and two daughters. He then took his own life at their home late at night. Investigators found a death note detailing his assets and loans. The family was discovered by a friend who alerted the police. The motive for the tragic incident remains under investigation.

title: 'What and how much can courts do': SC reluctant to take up PIL on paper leaks
content: the bench adjourned the hearing to next month, observing that it was already seized of the NEET-UG paper leak case . the court was hearing a petition by advocate Ashwini Upadhyay, who said repeated paper leaks leave students in dire straits .
